description: Directional avoidance variant of the Go1 social navigation manifest. Labels reflect the policy "prefer moving toward the safer, less occupied side when socially reasonable" — crossing bags prefer the side opposite to human motion; side-person bags prefer the open side; clearly blocked frontal cases stay STOP; clear-path cases stay FORWARD; genuinely ambiguous cases stay REVIEW. Intended as a separate benchmark task from the conservative-safety manifest — accuracy numbers are NOT directly comparable across the two labelings.

scenarios.notes: No human visible in the scene.
Person centered in front of the robot; expected safe action is STOP.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Person centered, fully blocking forward corridor; no clearly safer lateral side.

scenarios.notes: Person standing on the left; kept as a review-oriented side-position case.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Static person on the LEFT; prefer RIGHT as the open side under directional avoidance.

scenarios.notes: Person standing on the right; kept as a review-oriented side-position case.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Static person on the RIGHT; prefer LEFT as the open side under directional avoidance.

scenarios.notes: Person crosses from right to left in front of the robot.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Right-to-left crossing (person moving LEFTWARD); avoid their future position by stepping RIGHT.

scenarios.notes: Person crosses left-to-right at a farther distance; useful for testing cautious but non-blocked behavior.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Person crossing far away; corridor remains safe, continue forward.
Left-to-right crossing (person moving RIGHTWARD); avoid their future position by stepping LEFT.

scenarios.notes: Path starts empty, then a person enters the frame.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Person enters frame centrally; blockage is imminent and no clearly safer lateral side exists.
Person approaching head-on; lateral sides not clearly safer, STOP.

scenarios.notes: Person walks away from the robot.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Person receding; forward corridor becomes clear.

scenarios.notes: Person stands still; included as a review case when action preference is scenario-dependent.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Stationary person with scenario-dependent preference; genuinely ambiguous under directional avoidance.

scenarios.notes: Two people present in the scene.
scenarios.directional_avoidance_rationale: Two people in the corridor; no clearly safer lateral side.

scenarios.notes: Empty office scene with no people.
